results.statement: Exactly one LaTeX source input element.
Exactly one rendered-output container.
No list/repeater UI.
No export/download/copy/save controls.
No clipboard or download API usage.
No palette/snippets/examples UI.
Input element is a plain <textarea>.
Page loads KaTeX from a CDN.
Page does not load MathJax.
Renderer module calls KaTeX render API.
Valid LaTeX populates target with KaTeX-generated DOM.
Malformed LaTeX returns structured error without throwing.
results.detail: threw=false result={"ok":false,"message":"KaTeX parse error: Unexpected end of input in a macro argument, expected '}' at end of input: \\frac{1}{"}

results.statement: Output uses display-mode KaTeX (.katex-display).
Subscriber gets per-keystroke cumulative values.
On error, success output is cleared or marked stale.
No console errors on initial load.
Empty input -> no error, output empty.
Whitespace-only input behaves as empty.
Malformed input shows non-empty error and clears/marks-stale output.
Valid input renders KaTeX DOM and hides error region.
Zero error-level console messages on initial load.
Every edge_case_testing assertion passes against the integrated artifact.
